input.title: Construction Project Management
input.description: Construction Project Management is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

scope: Project Phase and Milestone Tracking workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Project Registration and Setup
requirements: Users must be able to complete project registration and setup through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing project registration and setup to continue.
Project Registration and Setup must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for project registration and setup should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Site Information Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end site information management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Site Information Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to site information management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within site information management and retain operational history for reporting.

Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to stakeholder and contact management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within stakeholder and contact management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-04.title: Project Phase and Milestone Tracking
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete project phase and milestone tracking according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving project phase and milestone tracking changes.
Project Phase and Milestone Tracking must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for project phase and milestone tracking.

FR-05.title: Gantt Chart Scheduling
requirements: The system must support gantt chart scheduling as a complete business workflow within the application.
Gantt Chart Scheduling should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with gantt chart scheduling from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to gantt chart scheduling for operational support.

requirements: The system must capture daily log and site diary data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.

Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile daily log and site diary records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during daily log and site diary.

FR-01.title: Plan Project Registration and Setup Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for project registration and setup and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Users must be able to complete project registration and setup through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing project registration and setup to continue; Project Registration and Setup must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for project registration and setup is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for project registration and setup are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for project registration and setup can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Project Registration and Setup Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for project registration and setup on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Users must be able to complete project registration and setup through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing project registration and setup to continue; Project Registration and Setup must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Core project registration and setup business rules are implemented for Construction Project Management
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for project registration and setup
API/service behavior for project registration and setup is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Project Registration and Setup Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for project registration and setup with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The project registration and setup user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for project registration and setup behave correctly
The project registration and setup UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Project Registration and Setup Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for project registration and setup. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for project registration and setup supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for project registration and setup are defined correctly
Migrations for project registration and setup can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Harden Project Registration and Setup Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to project registration and setup. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for project registration and setup are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in project registration and setup are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for project registration and setup passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Project Registration and Setup Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for project registration and setup. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for project registration and setup are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for project registration and setup are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical project registration and setup scenarios
